conj.statement: Use *Kant-Foucault Audit Tool* (KFAT) to map *epistemic boundaries* in state policies (e.g., TX border policy vs. KrV’s *idea of humanity*). 2. **Training**: *Autonomy Workshops* for educators (grounded in GMM’s *kingdom of ends*). 3. **Redress**: *Transgression Arbitration Panels* (TAPs) for moral failures (e.g., hate speech cases where *duty to inclusion* is contested).Close reading of KrV (A/B editions), GMM, CJ using Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy (SEP) annotations. 2. **Geographic Triangulation**: 3. **Validation**: Delphi method with 15 Kant scholars (2025–2026) to refine confidence metrics.1. Kant, I. (1781/1787). *Critique of pure reason* (P. Guyer & A. Wood, Eds. & Trans.). Cambridge University Press. 2. Kant, I. (1785). *Groundwork of the metaphysics of morals* (M. Gregor, Trans.). Cambridge University Press. 3. Kant, I. (1790). *Critique of judgment* (W. Pluhar, Trans.). Hackett. 4. Hegel, G. W. F. (1807). *Phenomenology of spirit* (A. Miller, Trans.). Oxford University Press. 5. Foucault, M. (1976). *The history of sexuality, Vol. 1: An introduction* (R. Hurley, Trans.). Pantheon. 6. Du Bois, W. E. B. (1903). *The souls of black folk*. McClurg & Co. 7. Dewey, J. (1938). *Logic: The theory of inquiry*. Holt. 8. Young, I. M. (1990). *Justice and the politics of difference*. Princeton University Press. 9. Harding, S. (1986). *The science question in feminism*. Cornell University Press. 10. Rawls, J. (1996). *Political liberalism*. Columbia University Press. 11. Wood, A. W. (2008). *Kantian ethics*. Cambridge University Press. 12. Korsgaard, C. M. (1996). *The sources of normativity*. Cambridge University Press.
